type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Yes, same file, real overlap: my task adds normalize_image_bytes() helper + refactors the image_to_base64 model_validator (lines ~67-84 of ImageBlock) to detect already-base64-encoded input before re-encoding/guessing mimetype. Your get_image_mimetype() lazy accessor sounds related/adjacent. Proposal: I implement first since my change restructures the validator body and adds the shared helper function; I'll send DONE with the exact diff. You then add get_image_mimetype() as a separate new method (not touching the validator internals) built on top of my change, reading the current file state before you start. If your accessor actually needs the validator to stop eagerly guessing mimetype, flag that now so we design the split together instead of clobbering. Sound OK?

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Confirmed overlap is real, not just adjacent: my normalize_image_bytes(image) returns (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing), and decoded_for_guessing REPLACES self.image as the input to filetype.guess(...) inside image_to_base64 (so I do touch lines ~78-82, not just line 82). Proposal to avoid clobbering: I go first. My version of image_to_base64 will look like: 'if not self.image: return self; encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image); if not self.image_mimetype: guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing); self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None; self.image = encoded_image; return self'. I will send DONE with the full diff once done (should be within a few minutes). After that, you rebase: change just the 'guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing); self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None' two lines into a call to your new self.get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing) method, and add that method elsewhere in the class. That way we each own distinct lines in the final file and neither of us clobbers the other's core logic. OK to proceed on this order?

content:
type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Implemented and tested (existing test_types.py 12/12 pass). Final image_to_base64 body:
    if not self.image:
        return self
    encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
    if not self.image_mimetype:
        guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing)
        self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
    self.image = encoded_image
    return self

normalize_image_bytes(image) is a new module-level function above MessageRole (around line 35), added 'import binascii' at top. You can now replace the two 'if not self.image_mimetype: guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing); self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None' lines with your self.get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing) call, and add that method elsewhere in ImageBlock. I won't touch this file further. Go ahead.
